channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: As Pakistan and Afghanistan declare truce, civilians in Kabul count the cost of war
item.description: At the Emergency Hospital, dozens crowded around a thick book to check the names of the victims killed in an airstrike on a rehabilitation center. The U.N. says over a hundred people were killed.
item.content:encoded:
At the Emergency Hospital, dozens crowded around a thick book to check the names of the victims killed in an airstrike on a rehabilitation center. The U.N. says over a hundred people were killed.

(Image credit: Fazelminallah Qazizai for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Fazelminallah Qazizai

item.title: Israel and Iran attack gas facilities. And, Cesar Chavez accused of sexual abuse, rape
item.description: Attacks on gas facilities by Israel and Iran have escalated the war and impacted global markets. And, renowned union leader and labor rights advocate Cesar Chavez is accused of sexual abuse and rape.
item.content:encoded:
Attacks on gas facilities by Israel and Iran have escalated the war and impacted global markets. And, renowned union leader and labor rights advocate Cesar Chavez is accused of sexual abuse and rape.

(Image credit: Oliver Contreras)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Japan's prime minister visits the White House under shadow of Iran war
item.description: Japan Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi will be the first U.S. ally to visit the White House since President Trump asked for help in sending ships to patrol the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
Japan Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi will be the first U.S. ally to visit the White House since President Trump asked for help in sending ships to patrol the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Tomohiro Ohsumi/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: New autism group meets to counter MAHA's 'ideological agenda'
item.description: Autism experts plan to convene in Washington Thursday to propose a research agenda at odds with the one endorsed by the Trump Administration.
item.content:encoded:
Autism experts plan to convene in Washington Thursday to propose a research agenda at odds with the one endorsed by the Trump Administration.

(Image credit: Tom Williams/CQ-Roll Call, Inc)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: Cursive is back. But should students be learning the skill?
item.description: A Virginia after-school cursive club went viral. More than two dozen states require cursive in their curriculums. Is it an effective learning tool or just nostalgia?
item.content:encoded:
A Virginia after-school cursive club went viral. More than two dozen states require cursive in their curriculums. Is it an effective learning tool or just nostalgia?

(Image credit: Anna Rose Layden for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Why Trump's attacks and threats to Iran's Kharg Island are a big deal
item.description: If the U.S. follows through on its threats to bomb the oil assets on Kharg Island, the war would likely escalate and oil prices would increase exponentially.
item.content:encoded:
If the U.S. follows through on its threats to bomb the oil assets on Kharg Island, the war would likely escalate and oil prices would increase exponentially.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: This tax season, there's a new deduction for interest on car loans
item.description: Taxpayers who purchased a new vehicle in 2025 may qualify for a new deduction on their taxes — even if they're not itemizing. But not everyone is eligible.
item.content:encoded:
Taxpayers who purchased a new vehicle in 2025 may qualify for a new deduction on their taxes — even if they're not itemizing. But not everyone is eligible.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Kick your tiredness with these 7 natural energy boosters
item.description: A full calendar doesn't mean you have to feel exhausted all the time. Experts share natural ways to boost energy and beat the constant battle of tiredness.
item.content:encoded: A full calendar doesn't mean you have to feel exhausted all the time. Experts share natural ways to boost energy and beat the constant battle of tiredness.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: As overseas terrorists regroup, is the Iran war increasing danger in the U.S.?
item.description: Investigators in the U.S. search for motives in three recent instances of targeted attacks, and whether they are related to the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded:
Investigators in the U.S. search for motives in three recent instances of targeted attacks, and whether they are related to the war in Iran.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef

item.title: It's called 'private credit' — and it could lead to big trouble on Wall Street
item.description: The risky lending business has been booming — but now its problems are becoming increasingly visible on Wall Street and beyond.
item.content:encoded:
The risky lending business has been booming — but now its problems are becoming increasingly visible on Wall Street and beyond.

(Image credit: ANGELA WEISS/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan
